entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum glaucum (L.) R. Br., a crop produced in India subject to various biotic constraints.

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW
entities.description: A major biotic constraint to pearl millet production caused by Sclerospora graminicola.

entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen responsible for causing downy mildew in pearl millet.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The geographic location where pearl millet production and the described research take place.

entities.name: HB 3
entities.description: A popular pearl millet hybrid that experienced substantial yield loss due to downy mildew epiphytotics in 1971.

entities.name: YIELD LOSS
entities.description: The reduction in crop productivity resulting from the impact of downy mildew.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, an organization conducting pathological research on pearl millet.

entities.name: ERGOT
entities.description: A disease of pearl millet caused by the pathogen Claviceps fusiformis.

entities.name: CLAVICEPS FUSIFORMIS
entities.description: The pathogen that causes ergot in pearl millet.

entities.name: SMUT
entities.description: A disease of pearl millet caused by the pathogen Moesziomyces penicillariae.

entities.name: MOESZIOMYCES PENICILLARIAE
entities.description: The pathogen that causes smut in pearl millet.

entities.name: RUST
entities.description: A disease of pearl millet caused by the pathogen Puccinia substriata var indica.

entities.name: PUCCINIA SUBSTRIATA VAR INDICA
entities.description: The pathogen that causes rust in pearl millet.

entities.name: SEEDBORNE ELIMINATION METHODS
entities.description: Techniques used to remove the downy mildew pathogen from seeds.

entities.name: SCREENING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Methods developed to identify pearl millet lines with resistance to downy mildew.

entities.name: MULTILOCATION EVALUATION
entities.description: The process of testing resistant lines across multiple locations to ensure stable resistance.

entities.name: STABLE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The consistent ability of a plant line to resist downy mildew across different environments.

entities.name: CHEMICAL AND CULTURAL METHODS
entities.description: Non-genetic methods used to supplement host-plant resistance for controlling downy mildew.

entities.name: HOST-PLANT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The primary method of disease control based on the genetic resistance of the pearl millet plant.

entities.name: ICAR
entities.description: Indian Council of Agricultural Research, a partner organization in the ongoing research with ICRISAT.
